Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

commit.message:
Fix write operations returning on error instead of only on success

Critical bug: All write operation recursive functions (update_element_content,
patch_element_settings, replace_element_full, delete_element_recursive,
extract_element) were returning on BOTH success AND error during recursion.

Problem:
```php
if ($result === true || is_wp_error($result)) {
    return $result;  // Returns element_not_found from child branch
}
```

This caused the search to stop when recursing into a child branch that didn't
contain the target element, instead of continuing to check sibling elements.

Symptoms:
- get_elementor_element on ID "db4aef4e" succeeds (read path works)
- patch_elementor_element on same ID fails with "Element not found"
- delete_element on same ID fails with "Element not found"
- replace_elementor_element on same ID fails with "Element not found"

Root cause: When searching for an element, if the function recursed into a
container's children array and didn't find the element there, it would return
the "element_not_found" error immediately instead of continuing to check the
next sibling container.

Fix:
```php
if ($result === true) {
    return true;  // Only return on success, continue on error
}
```

Now the recursion only returns when the element is FOUND. If not found in a
child branch, it continues checking siblings, just like find_element_by_id
(the read path) already does correctly.

Functions fixed:
- update_element_content() - Content updates
- patch_element_settings() - Settings patches
- replace_element_full() - Full element replacement
- delete_element_recursive() - Element deletion
- extract_element() - Element extraction for moves

This brings write operations in line with read operations (find_element_by_id,
which already had correct recursion logic).

Version: 1.1.0 (major fix, bumped minor version)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix collector crash when Frigate is temporarily unreachable

Replaced early return (which caused the container to exit and restart
in an infinite loop) with an exponential backoff retry loop. The daemon
now waits up to 5 min between attempts and stays alive until Frigate
becomes available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): prevent base failsafe excludes from suppressing real integration tests

The real-integration-tests profile was merging with the base failsafe
configuration, which has an explicit exclude for **/integration/real/**/*Test.java.
Since excludes win over includes in Maven Failsafe, the real tests were
silently excluded even when the profile was active.

Add combine.self="override" to the profile configuration so it fully
replaces the base config rather than merging with it.

commit.message:
Refactor: Split primitives/misc.rs (919 LOC, 21 classes) into logical submodules (BT-1102) (#1151)

## Summary

Splits the monolithic `primitives/misc.rs` (919 LOC, 21 unrelated
classes) into 5 domain-grouped submodules:

- **error_handling.rs** — Exception, StackFrame
- **system.rs** — File, Stream, System, Random, DateTime
- **reflection.rs** — CompiledMethod, JSON, Regex, Symbol
- **actor_types.rs** — Pid, Port, Reference, Future, FileHandle
- **value_types.rs** — Tuple, Set, Object, TestCase, TestRunner,
TestResult

Pure structural refactor — no logic changes. All 6173 existing tests
pass.

**Linear:** https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1102

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Reorganized primitive handling into focused modules (actor, system,
reflection, value types, error handling), splitting a large misc
implementation into smaller, targeted generators and updating the
primitive dispatch routing. No user-facing behavior changes; improves
maintainability and clarity of backend code generation.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
fix: deduct credits after AI call and add JSON tool-call fallback

Credit fix:
- Move useCredit() to after aiService.chat() completes so failed
  AI requests (bad API key, model unavailable, network error) no
  longer consume a credit. Credit errors after a successful response
  are non-fatal and logged but do not prevent showing the reply.

Tool-call fallback for all models:
- Update system prompt to allow JSON-in-<tool_call> format as a
  fallback when native function calling is unavailable, replacing
  the previous "never use pseudo tags" restriction.
- Extend parsePseudoToolCalls() in OpenRouterProvider to detect
  and parse the JSON format {"name":"...","arguments":{...}} in
  addition to the existing XML parameter format, so any model that
  follows the fallback instruction will have its tool calls executed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump protocol to 0.3.1 to fix missing ./validation subpath export

Published protocol@0.3.0 was missing the ./validation subpath in its
exports map, causing FUNCTION_INVOCATION_FAILED on all endpoints that
import from server-sdk (which depends on protocol/validation).

Also adds protocol as a direct dependency of demo at ^0.3.1.

commit.message:
Remove @capacitor-firebase/authentication, use signInWithPopup

The Capacitor Firebase Auth plugin requires CocoaPods (FirebaseAuth,
GoogleSignIn pods) which conflicts with the SPM-based iOS project,
causing a crash on launch. Use signInWithPopup for all platforms
instead — it works in WKWebView (iOS) and modern Android WebView.
